title: sabicea ingrata var. ingrata
intro: Sabicea ingrata var. ingrata, also known as 'Cassowary Apple', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of New Guinea and nearby islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Sabicea ingrata var. ingrata is often made into a tea or decoction, and its leaves can also be used to make a poultice for topical application.
